text: "The powers of magic are reserved for those with the potential to use them.  I sense no such potential in you."
speaker: zara

text: "May this aid you on your Quest!"
speaker: zara

text: As you read the spell scroll, the spell is ingrained in your mind.
speaker: narrator

text: Zara says, "I detect that you have not the money to purchase that item.  Do not toy with me!"
speaker: zara
say: I detect that you have not the money to purchase that item.  Do not toy with me!

text: Tall shelves with books and odds and ends suited to magic line the walls.  In front of you is a counter with a pentagram embossed on the front.  The ceiling and the far wall are mysterious indeed.  There is a strange symbol on the floor.
speaker: narrator

text: You see a wide variety of arcane objects.  For example:
speaker: narrator

text: A replacement part for a magical face-lift.
speaker: narrator

text: Books dealing with sorcery, necromancy and other occult subjects.
speaker: narrator

text: Bottles of mephitic potions.
speaker: narrator

text: Tinsel and chaff, very good for avoiding radar.
speaker: narrator

text: A hooded robe for when it rains in here.
speaker: narrator

text: Crystal balls, useful only to those initiated into the ways of witches.
speaker: narrator

text: A toaster oven, very rarely used (electricity not invented yet).
speaker: narrator

text: A frog.
speaker: narrator

text: Dried up Mantray (Manta jerky).
speaker: narrator

text: The ceiling is shrouded with clouds of smoke.
speaker: narrator

text: The floor is almost completely covered by a strange symbol.
speaker: narrator

text: You don't see any monsters here.
speaker: narrator

text: You don't see any living creatures.
speaker: narrator

text: This living creature must be some sort of familiar.  There is an uncanny look in its eyes, and you don't want to look at it too closely.  It definitely has magic.
speaker: narrator

text: The walls are covered with shelves full of interesting and very unusual objects.  The back wall behind the counter seems to be not a wall at all, but perhaps an extension of some unearthly place.
speaker: narrator

text: The titles are totally unfamiliar to you.
speaker: narrator

text: Aside from the pentagram on the front, there is nothing unusual about the counter.
speaker: narrator

text: Someone is watching you.
speaker: narrator

text: There is no one here to sell you anything.
speaker: narrator

text: Zara has a mysterious, unearthly look to her.  You have the feeling that she is not a person to trifle with.
speaker: narrator

text: "My name is Zara.  This is my shop."
speaker: zara

text: "I am both human and Faery Folk.  I draw my Power from both."
speaker: zara

text: "We are a people of Power and Magic, and live in the forests far beyond the mountains to the west."
speaker: zara

text: "Monsters stay out of town, and I stay in.  They are of no concern to me."
speaker: zara

text: "Damiano is my familiar and my companion.  We share our lives and our magic."
speaker: zara

text: "When you have mastered nine spells, have the power to cast them all, and proved yourself worthy by accomplishing a great deed, then you must undergo initiation to become a full Wizard."
speaker: zara

text: "There is a place in the distant south where you must journey, but first you must become a hero here."
speaker: zara

text: "A Wizard is one who both shapes Magic and is shaped by it.  Erasmus and I are the only Wizards in this valley."
speaker: zara

text: "There is much Magic in this world for those who know how to use it.  There is Magic in this little town, and a good deal of Magic in this valley."
speaker: zara

text: "What I sell here are merely tools to help those who have power.  You may purchase restorative potions and scrolls from which you may learn spells if you have the potential."
speaker: zara

text: "There is an Aura protecting this town from danger.  Within most of the town's walls, there can be no act of violence or cruel Magic.  Even so, it is prudent to avoid dark places."
speaker: zara

text: "An Aura is a Spell of protection surrounding something.  The town is still protected by the Aura cast by the great Spellcaster, Erana."
speaker: zara

text: "There is much Power in this valley, and it attracts those who use Magic.  I am here in Spielburg, Erasmus has his house on Zauberberg, and even Baba Yaga has her hut cooped up somewhere around here."
speaker: zara

text: "She is a powerful and wicked hag.  You would be wise to avoid her.  She cursed the Baron von Spielburg when he tried to drive her away."
speaker: zara

text: "This is Baba Yaga's curse, cast upon the Baron years ago:"
speaker: zara

text: "Upon von Spielburg and all his clan, this Curse I now demand:  What I will shall come full measure, so shall ye lose all that ye treasure."
speaker: zara

text: "There is always a way to break a curse; possibly Erasmus knows more about this one."
speaker: zara

text: "Baba Yaga's hut is Magical.  It stands on chicken legs, and you must know the rhyme to enter."
speaker: zara

text: "I have no interest in entering Baba Yaga's hut and therefore do not know the rhyme."
speaker: zara

text: "He was once the great leader of this land until he was foolish enough to anger Baba Yaga."
speaker: zara

text: "Erana was a powerful Spellcaster who lived long ago.  She brought peace to this valley.  Even now her spell protects this town from violence or foul Magic."
speaker: zara

text: "Her final resting place is due north of town, and it is a place of both safety and healing.  It is known as `Erana's Peace'"
speaker: zara

text: "Erasmus is a Wizard and a Spellcaster who knows much about this area.  He lives in a tower eastward of town.  He can be very helpful if approached properly, but he has a strange sense of humor."
speaker: zara

text: "It is a steep mountain to the northeast."
speaker: zara

text: "That is the Spell that lets you cast a Magical Flame upon your enemy.  The Spell Scroll will cost you 60 silver."
speaker: zara

text: "That is the Spell that lets you place magical energy upon a weapon.  The energy will be released as magical damage when you hit your enemy."
speaker: zara

text: "It is a Spell that allows you at first to unfasten simple locks and knots.  As you gain in skill, then you may open doors with it.  This Spell Scroll will cost you 30 silver."
speaker: zara

text: "The Fetch Spell allows you to lift or lower a lightweight object from a distance.  The Spell Scroll will cost you 40 silver."
speaker: zara

text: "We sell several Magical Spells on study scrolls.  You may purchase Flame Dart for 60 silver, Fetch for 40 silver, or the Open spell for 30 silver."
speaker: zara

text: "The Scrolls we sell are Magical.  You have but to read the Spell, and you will learn it."
speaker: zara

text: "I also know that you can find a wonderful Spell if you learn the secret of Erana's Peace."
speaker: zara

text: "Secrets are made to be kept."
speaker: zara

text: "We can sell you spell learning scrolls and enchanted potions."
speaker: zara

text: "The Healing Potion will cost you 50 silver.  It is made by the Healer outside of town."
speaker: zara

text: "Vigor Potions restore your Stamina after you have exerted yourself.  They cost 25 silver each."
speaker: zara

text: "Power is the essence of magic, that which the wizard shapes and is shaped by."
speaker: zara

text: "The Power Potion restores your magical energy, and costs 75 silver."
speaker: zara

text: "We have Healing, Vigor, and Power Potions."
speaker: zara

text: "Master the Arts Arcane, use those skills to vanquish the evil curse, and you will become a true Hero!"
speaker: zara

text: Just look at the shelves.
speaker: narrator

text: "We are entities of Magic and Power.  Matters such as that are of little importance."
speaker: zara

text: "We choose only to answer questions which interest us.  Do not persist with uninteresting questions."
speaker: zara

text: "We shall not waste our time further."
speaker: zara

text: "You may choose from the Fetch, Open, or Flame Dart Spells."
speaker: zara

text: "You may choose from the Healing, Vigor, or Power Potions."
speaker: zara

text: "I do not sell such things to such as you."
speaker: zara

text: Zara ignores you.  You would do better if you showed some respect by walking up to the counter before talking to her.
speaker: narrator

text: "So, you return to my shop.  Ask of me what you will, but keep to the point."
speaker: zara

text: "I am Zara, and my companion is Damiano."
speaker: zara

text: "The items in this shop are designed for those skilled in the use of magic.  We have very little for ones such as you who have not been initiated, but what we have could prove useful."
speaker: zara

text: It's a frog.
speaker: narrator

text: A pentagram, used in incantations to conjure spirits.
speaker: narrator

text: Impressive design, but merely decorative.
speaker: narrator

text: Paraphernalia and apparatus.
speaker: narrator

text: Accoutrements and equipment.
speaker: narrator

text: Darned if I know!
speaker: narrator

text: Odds and ends.
speaker: narrator

text: Ends and odds.
speaker: narrator

text: Things and stuff.
speaker: narrator

text: Jeff's jimcracks.
speaker: narrator

text: Magical junk and mystical garbage.
speaker: narrator

text: This dark, seedy place is less than you expected of a Thieves' Guild.
speaker: narrator

text: The man seated by the table greets you with a scowl;  Crusher is his usual, amiable self;  and you can't quite make out what lurks behind that massive door.
speaker: narrator

text: You feel uneasy about the sinister mood here.
speaker: narrator

text: "I don't have time to talk to unlicensed scum.  Pay your guild license fee, and then we can talk."
speaker: thief-guildmaster

text: "What do you think this is, your home?  Crusher, deal with this deadbeat."
speaker: thief-guildmaster

text: The door is locked, and Boris won't let you in.
speaker: narrator

text: Picking locks in the Thieves' Guild is a singularly stupid idea.
speaker: narrator

text: Crusher ignores you.
speaker: narrator

text: "A Thief's license will cost you 25 silvers.  You will need a license to use any of our services."
speaker: boris

text: "Perhaps I could sell you a good lockpick for fifteen silvers, or a wonderful Acme Thief Tool Kit for one hundred silvers."
speaker: boris

text: "I have no time for idle talk.  Why don't you do something constructive like stealing Otto's yo-yo?"
speaker: unsure-boris

text: "I can't hear you.  Please come a little closer to the window."
speaker: boris

text: From his expensive clothes to his carefully trimmed mustache, there is an air of sophistication to this villain.
speaker: narrator

text: Crusher is his usual, amiable self (every bit the Goon).
speaker: narrator

text: The opening in the door is covered with a wire grate, but there is space below it where objects can be passed through.  The mysterious-looking man on the other side is Boris, the cashier and fence for the Guild.
speaker: narrator

text: The ladder up to the tavern is very sturdy.  It has to be to support Crusher's weight.
speaker: narrator

text: It looks like an oversized, well-used dartboard.
speaker: narrator

text: There sure are a lot of daggers in the board.  You wonder where the Chief Thief keeps them all.
speaker: narrator

text: The shadowy figure behind the door is Boris, the cashier and fence for the Guild.
speaker: narrator

text: The floor is surprisingly clean, compared to that of the bar upstairs.
speaker: narrator

text: The plaster is chipping in several places.
speaker: narrator

text: You can tell where the bar is by the seep marks of spilled beer.
speaker: narrator

text: The daggers are nicely made, and apparently designed for throwing.  Besides the Chief Thief's seemingly endless collection, there are two matched sets with differently-colored hilts for playing Dag-Nab-It.
speaker: narrator

text: Even thieves get thirsty.
speaker: narrator

text: You're probably wondering if there is any honor among thieves.
speaker: narrator

text:
Stealing from a dagger-throwing thief?
That's a dangerous (and stupid) idea!
speaker: narrator

text: "Go up to the window, please."
speaker: boris

text: "Don't bother.  You've already paid, stupid."
speaker: boris

text: "Welcome to the Thieves' Guild Union, Local 1313."
speaker: boris

text: "I'm sorry, but you don't seem to have enough silver for your license.  Come back when you qualify."
speaker: boris

text: "Our very special items are reserved for our guild members.  No scabs allowed."
speaker: boris

text: "There you are."
speaker: boris

text: "We only deal in cash here."
speaker: boris

text: "We only sell lockpicks and Thief Toolkits here."
speaker: boris

text: "We buy only from our members."
speaker: boris

text: You can't fence something you don't have.
speaker: narrator

text: "We have no interest in those."
speaker: boris

text: "Let's see now... deducting the Guild cut, and allowing for resale markup, that comes to..."
speaker: boris

text: "%d silvers.  Here you are."
speaker: boris

text: You now have %d gold and %d silver coins.
speaker: narrator

text: "A dagger is a thief's best friend, and throwing from a distance is the safest and best way to use it.  If you think you are good enough, perhaps we could play a little game."
speaker: thief-guildmaster

text: "We use the specially designed Dag-Nab-It board you can see there on the wall."
speaker: thief-guildmaster

text: "We could start small, perhaps five silvers a game, perhaps even less.  Of course, we can wager more if you think you can take me."
speaker: thief-guildmaster

text: "There are only a few members of our Guild still around here.  With the snow and the brigands keeping away the tourists, it's hard to earn a decent living."
speaker: thief-guildmaster

text: "Where else but here can one mingle freely with others of our chosen profession, sharing tips and secrets?  Where else can you safely fence stolen items?"
speaker: thief-guildmaster

text: "Where else can you ask questions like these and not get thrown into jail or attacked?  The Guild is truly your home away from home."
speaker: thief-guildmaster

text: "Those who do not pay their union dues are the lowest form of life."
speaker: thief-guildmaster

text: "My advice is, don't get caught."
speaker: thief-guildmaster

text: "Now that would be telling."
speaker: thief-guildmaster

text: "Just give your stolen items to Boris and you will be suitably recompensed.  Or, in terms more suited to your apparent intellect, you get cash for your loot."
speaker: thief-guildmaster

text: "Boris is our accountant."
speaker: thief-guildmaster

text: "Crusher is our personnel manager."
speaker: thief-guildmaster

text: "Otto is the Sheriff's goon.  He shouldn't give you any trouble unless you happen to stand in his way."
speaker: thief-guildmaster

text: "The Sheriff provides a useful service in this town.  He arrests anyone too stupid or unlucky to be a good thief."
speaker: thief-guildmaster

text: "I am known as 'The Chief'.  But you can call me 'Chief'."
speaker: thief-guildmaster

text: "These non-union cretins are giving thieves a bad name.  I'm sure they have a spy planted in our organization, but I don't know who it is."
speaker: thief-guildmaster

text: "There is certainly a traitor in our midst."
speaker: thief-guildmaster

text: "There are a few places around town where a clever and skilled thief can make some silver.  Perhaps even you could steal something."
speaker: thief-guildmaster

text: "I have had beginners before, but this Guild reached a new low when you joined.  Child, you are a thief.  Stealing is how you make a living.  So go out there and steal something and hopefully you'll get, umm, that is, you *won't* get caught."
speaker: thief-guildmaster

text: "We will gladly sell you a lock pick for a modest fee.  However, for the true professional, nothing will do but the Guild-Approved Acme Thief Tool Kit.  This kit will allow you to pick anything with a little practice, and is a real steal at only one hundred silvers.  Just talk to Boris."
speaker: thief-guildmaster

text: "Now that has to be one of the stupidest things anyone has ever asked me."
speaker: thief-guildmaster

text: "Beginners!  Why do they keep giving me beginners?"
speaker: thief-guildmaster

text: "I tell you, Crusher, that a thief of my stature and abilities should be stuck in a podunk town besieged by brigands who aren't even Union Members is the height of injustice!"
speaker: thief-guildmaster

text: "It will cost you 25 silvers to work in town.  Just pay Boris at the door.  If you don't have the cash, go steal something."
speaker: thief-guildmaster

text: You reach for your money but come up empty-handed.  Unable to pay for your goods, you put the merchandise back.
speaker: narrator

text: This house is the same color as the Sheriff's office around the corner.
speaker: narrator

text: A simple but prosperous-looking edifice it is.  It looks like it might provide some staples that would prove useful to a traveler.
speaker: narrator

text: It is but a small stall, used for the daily sale of whatever local produce has not been confiscated by the brigands.
speaker: narrator

text: There is no response.  The store must be closed for the evening.
speaker: narrator

text: There is no one around.  The markets are closed for the night.
speaker: narrator

text: All of the produce has been taken in for the night.
speaker: narrator

text: The creature in charge of the Farmer's Mart is a rather lovely and shy-looking young centaur.
speaker: narrator

text: The selection is meagre and the appearance is not of the best, but the aromas are strangely enticing.  A barrel contains some small apples, and there are several types of vegetables on the counter.
speaker: narrator

text: This appears to be a Market street.  In the corner is a fruit and vegetable stand, or Farmer's Mart.  Next to it is a dry goods store, and next to that is a house.  Across the street you see the back of the barber's shop and the Sheriff's office.
speaker: narrator

text: You see the backs of buildings.
speaker: narrator

text: Unlike an Ogre, YOU can read!
speaker: narrator

text: The lock appears to be an extra heavy-duty model.
speaker: narrator

text: The door appears to be locked, but the lock is of a simple type.
speaker: narrator

text: "I will be happy to take your silver.  Please pick out what you want."
speaker: hilde

text: "As you wish, sir."
speaker: hilde

text: The pretty young centaur looks at you and says, "Good day, and welcome to Spielburg!  Do you wish to buy some nice fresh fruits or vegetables?"
speaker: hilde
say: Good day, and welcome to Spielburg!  Do you wish to buy some nice fresh fruits or vegetables?

text: "I am Hilde, daughter of Heinrich Pferdefedern, the farmer."
speaker: hilde

text: "My father and I are the only centaurs in this valley."
speaker: hilde

text: "We have many kinds of fresh vegetables for sale today.  There are lettuce, carrots, potatoes, and turnips right from the ground at five for a silver.  They are very good for you!"
speaker: hilde

text: "It is much too early in the season for those.  Sorry."
speaker: hilde

text: "I'm sorry, but the cherry harvest is not yet in.  But we will have very nice cherries two months from now."
speaker: hilde

text: "There are still some apples left from last season in the barrel.  You may buy ten for a silver, since they are so small.  We will have fresh cherries in a couple of months."
speaker: hilde

text: "They have twice robbed my Father of his money, and they have tried to steal our food this winter.  That is because we farm outside of town.  They would not dare to try that in town.  The Sheriff would stop them fast!"
speaker: hilde

text: "Many robbers have run up to Father as he trotted back to town.  My Father tried to fight, but they hurt him badly.  They always ran away to the west."
speaker: hilde

text: "We have some land to the north of town.  It isn't very big, but we grow many things.  You should be here during the harvest time.  Then you would see many fruits and vegetables."
speaker: hilde

text: "You know...in the autumn!  But then, perhaps you have never been a farmer."
speaker: hilde

text: "The Healer lives in a nice little cottage on the North road, near my Father's fields.  She is a very nice woman."
speaker: hilde

text: "Thank you, but my Father thinks I am too young.  He would not permit it.  But perhaps next year..."
speaker: hilde

text: "My Father is Heinrich Pferdefedern, who works our farm just north of town."
speaker: hilde

text: "I am sorry.  I do not get about very much, and I do not really know about many things."
speaker: hilde

text: If you want to buy something, go over to the vendor.
speaker: narrator

text: You pick out the freshest-looking vegetables you can find, and pay the lady a silver piece.
speaker: narrator

text: You carefully select ten of the best apples from the barrel, and pay the lady a silver piece.
speaker: narrator

text: "As I told you, sir, it is too early in the season.  You would not want to buy any."
speaker: hilde

text: "I'm afraid I can't help you with that, sir."
speaker: hilde

text: "Just pick out what you would like to purchase, then you can pay me."
speaker: hilde

text: However, this was not a good place to sleep.  You're stiff and sore all over and your wallet feels very light.  You give the Hilde the centaur a suspicious look, but you are too sore to pursue the matter.
speaker: narrator

text: "I'm sorry, but I had to move you out of my stall!  It is very dangerous to sleep in the street at night.  I fear you have been robbed."
speaker: hilde

text: The smell of lavender and dust fills your nose as you walk in.  This reminds you of a great aunt's house you once visited.  The couch looks every bit as uncomfortable as your aunt's.
speaker: narrator

text: There is a covered birdcage near the stairs, and a knitting basket beside the couch.

text: The Little Old Lady's cries finally bring the Sheriff and Otto to her rescue.  They find you pinned to the floor, unconscious, dreaming deliriously of death by sandpaper.
speaker: narrator

text: When the Little Old Lady awakes to see what's going on, you have to concede to her (through lips that are as raw as hamburger) that you've been licked!  She summons the Sheriff and his goon, Otto.

text: "You can't imagine how good it feels to know who I am again.  After all these years, to remember my name is Elsa von Spielburg!"

text: "I used to wish I was not a brigand's child but actually an enchanted princess.  Now I know that I really was enchanted, and I don't have to be a brigand any more!"
speaker: elsa

text: "Thank you so much for freeing me."

text: "I've got to hurry home to father and get his guards to capture the rest of the brigands before they manage to escape with the treasure.  It must be returned to the rightful owners."

text: "Please don't bore me, young adventurer.  Ask me about something interesting to me."

text: "The sword is the finest of all weapons.  It requires an equal measure of skill and strength."
speaker: weapon-master

text: "I am the Weapon Master."
speaker: weapon-master

text: "You will find them very skilled at fighting.  After all, I am the one who trains all of the guards at this castle."
speaker: weapon-master

text: "The Baron had the wisdom to recognize greatness when I presented myself.  Thus he made me Weapon Master."
speaker: weapon-master

text: "The Baronet had some minor talent with weapons, but he was too impatient.  If he had but listened and learned what I teach, he would never have been lost."
speaker: weapon-master

text: "I never use a shield or armor under most circumstances.  It only gets in the way of my agility and dodging ability.  It is best used by those who are strong, but not swift or agile."
speaker: weapon-master

text: "Practice is the way to refine and improve one's skills."
speaker: weapon-master

text: "If you are addressing me, sir, you must respect me enough to address me to my face!"

text: "Well, it's dark and damp and the waters roars sos there's not a moment's quiet.  The ceiling drips and the walls are slimy and it's always cold.  But, oh well, sez I, it's 'ome."

text: "A 'ermit is one wot lives alone, far away from them wot talks too much or asks too many questions.  'Ermits are shy, quiet types wot don't say much.  Me brother 'Arry 'ardly says a word in a year.  Me sister 'Ortense 'asn't spoken since she were six.  I never talks at all.  Yes, we 'ermits nose how to keep a mouth shut."
speaker: hermit

text: "Me family lives pretty far apart from each other. We're all 'ermits, you know."
speaker: hermit

text: "I'm 'enry.  'Enry the eighth I is.  Me farther was an 'Enry and 'is farther was an 'Enry and 'is farther was an 'Enry and 'is farther was an 'Enry and 'is farther was an 'Enry and 'is farther was an 'Enry and 'is farther was an 'Enry. And every one was an 'ermit."
speaker: hermit

text: "I likes to play the game.  I can show you how to play, if you likes."
speaker: hermit

text: "It's the spell wot sets off uther spells."
speaker: hermit

text: "Like when you wants to see the ladder, you casts a trigger and there you 'ave it, large as life."
speaker: hermit

text: "Course, you gots to 'ave a spell on the invisible ladder to make it visible first.  But it 'as its uses."
speaker: hermit

text: "I thinks I 'ave a scroll with the spell, if you be knowing sum magic, that is."
speaker: hermit

text: "The spells wot I nose is pretty simple maybe, but I like 'em."
speaker: hermit

text: "I can make me ladder show up and me door close and even get rid o' pests using the trigger spell, you see."
speaker: hermit

text: "I nose sum magic.  Erasmus the wizard tot me sum spells."
speaker: hermit

text: "He's wot put the spell on me ladder sos the brigands don't get up.  Them wot climbs th' rocks gets a door opening in their nose."
speaker: hermit

text: "This 'ere's wot's called the Flying Falls."
speaker: hermit

text: "I've doon sum flying falls arund 'ere meself. That porch gets slippery sumtimes.  He, heh, he."
speaker: hermit

text: From the size of the ancient oak kegs, you surmise that the drinking of large quantities of beverages is the main pastime in this establishment.
speaker: narrator

text: As dirt goes, it's pretty dirty.
speaker: narrator

text: It's in the kegs.
speaker: narrator

text: Mighty frothy. Looks OK.
speaker: narrator

text: Looks lean, mean, and green.
speaker: narrator

text: The fumes hurt your eyes.
speaker: narrator

text: It doesn't look like the bartender ever wipes up spills, and the glasses are truly filthy.
speaker: narrator

text: The man on the west side of the table is wearing a blood-stained apron, like a butcher. The man on the east has on a baker's hat. They seem to be deeply involved in their game.
speaker: narrator

text: You can't see the game very well from where you are.
speaker: narrator

text: They seem to be using an "Old Maid" deck.
speaker: narrator

text: There are several.
speaker: narrator

text: That's a mighty unusual keg, there.
speaker: narrator

text: That looks mighty nasty!
speaker: narrator

text: There are three bar stools at the bar.
speaker: narrator

text: It looks like something you'd expect to see in a tavern, but a lot dirtier.
speaker: narrator

text: The grimy window lets little light into this tavern.  It smells like stale ale and other more unpleasant things.  The floor is covered with dirt, and the bar with sticky beer.
speaker: narrator

text: Smoke appears to be rising from the center cask behind the bar.  To your right, two gamblers are playing cards.
speaker: narrator

text: The bartender glares at you as you enter, and so does the ugly goon on the left. You get the impression that you are not welcome.
speaker: narrator

text: There seems to be something slimy spilled on top of the stool, and it is eating into the wood.  You think perhaps you should sit on the next stool over.
speaker: narrator

text: CONGRATULATIONS!!  You have successfully completed "Quest for Glory I:  So You Want To Be A Hero" with the maximum possible score, 500 of 500!!  We welcome you to the ranks of the few, the proud, the True Heroes!
speaker: narrator

text: Congratulations!  You have successfully completed "Quest for Glory I:  So You Want To Be A Hero."  Your final score was %d of 500 possible Puzzle Points.
speaker: narrator

text: If you have not already done so, we encourage you to play "Quest for Glory I" again with the other two character types; many of the puzzles are different, or have alternate solutions.

text:
In the meantime, you are already a winner!  Please insert a writeable disk in your floppy drive to save your winning Hero for use in
"Quest for Glory II:  Trial By Fire."

Thank you for playing "Quest for Glory I:  So You Want To Be A Hero," and congratulations again on winning.  We'll see you again soon in
"Quest for Glory II:  Trial By Fire."

text: Wow!  You're Really A Hero!  [score %d of 500]
speaker: narrator

text: You are in a clearing at the edge of the forest.  You are faced with impassably steep, rocky escarpments on two sides.
speaker: narrator

text: The late frosts of winter give way to to the greenery of spring.  There is the crisp smell of cedar in the air.  This forest seems to be unusually still and quiet.
speaker: narrator

text: The meadow clearing in which you stand is grassy and littered with blown pine needles and small mossy rocks.
speaker: narrator

text: Pine needles, moss, and early spring grasses cover the forest floor.
speaker: narrator

text: The evening is clear and the stars are bright.  Dark clouds pass over the moon.
speaker: narrator

text: The low-lying bushes form tight tangles of shrubbery between the trees.
speaker: narrator

text: A tumbled pattern of rocks and boulders leads upwards to the steep cliffs and peaks of the mountain range surrounding this small valley.
speaker: narrator

text: The valley floor is covered with fine, moist soil with few rocks.
speaker: narrator

text: Looking through the surrounding woods, you occasionally are able to catch a glimpse of the snow-capped mountain peaks.
speaker: narrator

text: There are no caves here.
speaker: narrator

text: Climbing would serve no purpose right now.
speaker: narrator

text: Let's see how much fun you have pulling splinters from your lips.
speaker: narrator

text: "Back so soon?"
speaker: baba-yaga

text:
"Spirits of Mist
And Creatures of Bog:
Transform my guest
To the shape of a Frog."
speaker: baba-yaga

text:
"This I vow:
Stay there now!"
speaker: baba-yaga

text: Once again you're a frog, and once again you can't move.  You find it very exasperating!
speaker: narrator

text: "Yum, Yum.  Froggie Frappe!"
speaker: baba-yaga

text: "Did you bring me my mandrake like you promised?"
speaker: baba-yaga

text: As you make a feeble croaking sound, you try to nod your head.
speaker: narrator

text: "Well?  Where is it?"
speaker: baba-yaga

text: "What's the matter?  Got a frog in your throat?"
speaker: baba-yaga

text: "I suppose I'll have to turn you back into whatever it is that you were.  Pity!  You're much more appetizing this way."
speaker: baba-yaga

text:
"Creatures of Bog
And Spirits of Fog:
Return the true form
To this rather dumb Frog."
speaker: baba-yaga

text: "Now, did you put it in your backpack?"
speaker: baba-yaga

text: "Ah!  Here it is."
speaker: baba-yaga

text: "Kids!  We have it!"
speaker: baba-yaga

text: "That's it!  The final ingredient.  Now we can make our greatest creation..."
speaker: baba-yaga

text: "MANDRAKE MOUSSE!"
speaker: baba-yaga

text: "What's that, children?  You think we should reward our lackey here?"
speaker: baba-yaga

text: "Very well, ex-frog.  I'll let you live this time.  Next time, though, it's frog legs for sure!"
speaker: baba-yaga

text: "So...Go!"
speaker: baba-yaga

text: The great house of Baron Stefan von Spielburg rises on a low hillock over the courtyard and castle walls.
speaker: narrator

text: There are guard towers at regular intervals around the castle wall.
speaker: narrator

text: The gatehouse is a massive structure with a portcullis closing off access to the castle.
speaker: narrator

text: It's a gate that raises and lowers.
speaker: narrator

text: The castle walls are thick and massive.  There is a walkway around the rampart for the guards to patrol.
speaker: narrator

text: The main courtyard is a broad, grassy area with a large octagonal area of flagstone paving in the center.
speaker: narrator

text: The flagstones are from a rock quarry in eastern Germany.
speaker: narrator

text: Well...maybe the flagstones are from western Germany.
speaker: narrator

text: Flagstones from Europe?
speaker: narrator

text: Granite from our very own moutains?
speaker: narrator

text: I don't know!
speaker: narrator

text: The flying Wallendas have nothing on you.
speaker: narrator

text: No time for that again (although it WAS fun).  Just get the heck outta here.
speaker: narrator

text: You're not in a good spot.
speaker: narrator

text: It's too late for that.
speaker: narrator

text: He's been drinking Troll's sweat all night.
speaker: narrator

text: "Brigands over all!"  Egotistical bunch, aren't they?
speaker: narrator

text: The rope holds up the chandelier.  It's used to lower the chandelier so that it can be lit.
speaker: narrator

text: Sturdy-looking device for holding candles.
speaker: narrator

text: Chandelier, used to hold up the rope.
speaker: narrator

text: You close and bar the door.
speaker: narrator

text: You're hopelessly outnumbered.  You should have left this room when you had a chance.
speaker: narrator

text: You are in a clearing at the edge of the forest. The road you have been following is completely blocked by a very recent avalanche.
speaker: narrator

text: It appears that a very recent avalanche has sealed off the road leading out of the valley.
speaker: narrator

text: The meadow clearing in which you stand is grassy; the road is densely packed and slightly muddy.
speaker: narrator

text: A tumbled pattern of rocks can be seen at the forest's edge.
speaker: narrator

text: The ragged peaks of the snow-covered mountains can be seen clearly. The pass, however, is blocked by very recent avalanches.
speaker: narrator

text: "Too much for you?  I guess you should work on your magic.  Maybe next time you'll be more of a challenge."
speaker: erasmus

text: "Guess I still have a knack for the old Maze game."
speaker: erasmus

text: "Well, you seem to have beginner's luck.  It only took you %d game%s to win.  Hmph.  It usually takes at least %d tries for anyone less than a full Wizard to defeat me."
speaker: erasmus

text: "As I promised, I shall now teach you the spell known as `Erasmus' Razzle Dazzle'."
speaker: erasmus

text: "Well!  You don't have to rub it in, you know!"
speaker: erasmus
